name: Timeline Scrubber
description: Horizontal multi-track timeline with draggable scrubber handle, scene markers, keyframe dots, and zoom controls for video/audio editing workflows.

video.description: Multi-track video timeline with frame thumbnails, stacked audio tracks, scene markers, and full keyframe support for editing workflows

audio.description: Audio-focused timeline rendering waveform envelopes across multiple stems with beat grid alignment and region editing

simple.description: Single track timeline with scrubber and basic zoom, suitable for preview players and lightweight review tools

visual_brief: A horizontal multi-track timeline stacked vertically with a fixed-position time ruler at top showing hours:minutes:seconds:frames tick marks. Video tracks render sequential frame thumbnails (approximately 80x45px each) at regular intervals matching zoom level. Audio tracks show symmetrical amplitude waveform envelopes in accent color against a muted background. A prominent vertical scrubber line (2px wide, primary color) spans all tracks with a triangular playhead handle at top that the user drags left-right to seek. Scene markers appear as colored flags above the ruler at scene-change timestamps, each with a 12px colored pennant shape. Keyframes render as 8px rotated-square diamond shapes on their associated track rows, connected by thin lines showing interpolation. Zoom controls sit in a small pill at bottom-right with +/-/fit buttons. Current time displays in monospace font at top-left (e.g. 00:02:14:07). Beat/frame grid lines run vertically through all tracks at zoom-appropriate density.

slots.time-ruler: Horizontal ruler at the top showing time markers with major ticks (seconds) and minor ticks (frames). Label format depends on zoom level: frames, seconds, or minutes. Monospace font for time labels. Scrolls horizontally in sync with track content.
slots.track-stack: Vertical stack of track rows. Each row is 64px tall for video tracks (to accommodate thumbnails) or 48px tall for audio tracks. Rows have a track header on the left (120px fixed) showing track name, mute/solo, and lock toggles, with the waveform/thumbnail area filling remaining width.
slots.scrubber: Vertical playhead line spanning all tracks, draggable via triangular handle at top. Must use pointer-events to capture drag; updates time via onChange. Shows current-time tooltip near the handle while dragging.
slots.scene-markers: Colored flag markers positioned absolutely above the ruler at scene-change timestamps. Hover shows scene name tooltip. Clicking jumps the scrubber to the marker position.
slots.keyframes: Diamond-shaped dots on individual track rows at automation keyframe timestamps. Selectable, draggable, connected by interpolation lines that reflect curve type (linear, bezier, hold).
slots.zoom-controls: Small pill-shaped toolbar anchored bottom-right with zoom-in, zoom-out, and fit-to-content buttons. Icon-only with tooltips.

responsive.mobile: Track headers collapse to 48px wide icon-only. Zoom controls hidden, replaced by pinch gesture. Thumbnails downscaled to 40x22px. Scene markers render as colored dots without labels.
responsive.tablet: Standard layout with slightly reduced track heights (52px video, 40px audio). Touch-drag for scrubber and two-finger pinch for zoom.
responsive.desktop: Full layout with all controls visible and keyboard shortcuts enabled for playback and zoom navigation.

layout_hints.playhead_priority: The scrubber and time ruler should be the dominant orientation anchors. Tracks, keyframes, and markers all support that shared temporal frame.
layout_hints.track_hierarchy: Video, audio, and annotation tracks should remain clearly separated so the user always understands what layer they are editing or reviewing.
layout_hints.zoom_discipline: As zoom changes, maintain the relationship between ruler, markers, and media blocks instead of letting one layer drift out of sync with the others.

layout_hints.playhead_priority: The scrubber and time ruler should be the dominant orientation anchors. Tracks, keyframes, and markers all support that shared temporal frame.
layout_hints.track_hierarchy: Video, audio, and annotation tracks should remain clearly separated so the user always understands what layer they are editing or reviewing.
layout_hints.zoom_discipline: As zoom changes, maintain the relationship between ruler, markers, and media blocks instead of letting one layer drift out of sync with the others.

micro.scrubber-drag: scrubber x-position follows pointer at 60fps with no easing

transitions.zoom-change: track-width transition 250ms ease-in-out with thumbnail re-rendering
transitions.marker-add: scale(0) to scale(1) with bounce 200ms spring

ambient.playhead-playing: scrubber advances continuously at playback rate with subtle glow pulse

composition.TrackStack: Container > TrackRow*(d-surface) > [TrackHeader + TrackContent > (FrameThumbnails | Waveform) + KeyframeDot* + SceneMarker*]

keyboard: M: add scene marker at playhead
